_why: Complete tool orchestration configuration. Defines routing logic, cost accounting, batching rules, and failure strategies for all tools available to AI-OS runtime.
orchestration_principles: Use the minimum number of tools required to answer accurately
Every tool call consumes RPM quota — treat it as a scarce resource
Batch all tool results before the NIM inference call
Never call a tool whose result will not change the final answer
Max 4 tool calls per turn (reserves 1 RPM unit for NIM inference)

github.name: GitHub MCP
trigger: query is about a specific repository by owner/name
query asks about a specific commit SHA, branch, or PR number
query asks to read, create, or update a file in a repo
query asks for issue or PR status
query requires a code search in a specific repo
skip: general programming questions not tied to a repo
debugging questions about code the user has pasted directly
github.failure_strategy: acknowledge error, state which repo data is missing, proceed without it

brain_memory.name: Brain Memory MCP
trigger: user references a prior decision from a previous session
user references a project by name with no other context in current session
answer quality materially improves from knowing user preferences
user asks to remember something
skip: generic factual questions with no personal context dependency
first turn greeting with no project reference
user has already provided all needed context in current session
brain_memory.failure_strategy: skip memory, proceed without user context, do not surface error to user

web_search.name: Web Search
trigger: query requires information from last 30 days
query asks about current software version or release
query asks about a live system status or incident
query explicitly requests current information
skip: stable conceptual knowledge available in model training
project-specific questions better answered by RAG
questions about the AI-OS project itself (use GitHub tool)
web_search.failure_strategy: fall back to model knowledge with [verify] flag

knowledge_rag.name: Knowledge (RAG)
trigger: a relevant collection is loaded
query is about library API usage with version specificity
query is about codebase-specific patterns
query asks 'how does X work in this project'
skip: query is about current events (use web search)
prefer_over_web_search_when: collection contains content more authoritative than web results
knowledge_rag.failure_strategy: fall back to model knowledge with [model knowledge — verify with current docs] flag

calculator.name: Calculator
trigger: multi-step arithmetic where intermediate error would compound
statistical derivations
unit conversions requiring precision
token budget calculations
RPM accounting
skip: rough estimates where precision is not required
calculator.failure_strategy: state arithmetic inline with explicit step-by-step, flag as unverified

future_mcp_tools._why: AI-OS is designed to be extensible. New MCP tools follow the same routing, cost, and batching rules.
onboarding_requirements: Add routing entry to compiled_prompt (rebuild via prompt_compiler)
